rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Training platform = Claude Code (CLI + Desktop) + Cowork.
Verify capabilities BEFORE asserting, not after.
Known correct Claude Code facts (easy to get wrong).
Every prompt block gets a one-sentence action lead-in.
rules_evaluated.evidence: line:7 — {{prompt:ae101-agent-loop-ascii}} has no individual lead-in; line:9 — {{prompt:ae101-agent-loop-tools-list}} has no individual lead-in; line:11 — {{prompt:ae101-agent-loop-tool-injection}} has no individual lead-in. Collective framing 'Three prompts to make the loop concrete...' does not satisfy per-prompt lead-in requirement.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Add a one-sentence action lead-in before each marker, e.g. 'Ask Claude to draw the loop as an ASCII diagram.' / 'Ask Claude to list its available tools.' / 'Ask Claude to explain how it knows its tools and how MCP connectors join the set.'

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Canonical presentation shape — lead-in, label with destination, blank line, fenced block.
Installed Claude Code Skills invoke by name, never by path.
Markdown shout inside fenced prompt blocks is dead weight in Claude Code.
Prompts live in the registry; module/exercise/lecture files reference them by marker.
Verification machinery needs a smoke-test before being trusted.

platform_claims_checked.claim: Claude Code is one harness; Cursor, Cline, Gemini CLI are others.
the LLM receives context (system prompt, conversation, tool results), decides on the next step, either responds to you or calls a tool
The agent harness is the surrounding code that runs the loop, exposes tool definitions to the LLM, executes the tool calls, feeds results back in
how MCP-connector tools get injected into the same set
